title: Pouvoirs sud-africains

pages.text:
FULL POHER

WHEREAS the Governments of the United Kingdom

of Great Eritain and Northern Ireland, Canada, Austra- Ila, New

Zealand, the union of South Africa, Indla

and Pakistan, on the one hand, and the Governnent of

France, on the other hand, are destrous of concluding an Agreement in respect of the centeries, graves and memorials of members of the armed forces of the

Commonwealth countries who fell in the War of 1939-1945 and whose remains rest in French territory;

AND WHEREAS the Government of the Union of

South Africa have exanined the aforesaid Agreement, and deen it desirable that the agreement be signed and that a fit and proper person be granted full power to effect such signature on behalf of the Government of the Union of South Africa;

NOW, THEREFORE, I, DANIEL FRANÇOIS MALAN,

Prime Minister and Minister of External Affairs of the

Union of South Africa, do hereby certify and declare that

HARRY THOMSON

ANDREWS,

Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentlary of the

Union of south Airica in France, has been authorised and appolated, and 15 by these presents authorised and appointed, with full power to sign the aforementioned

•

Agreement on behalf of the Governuent of the Union of

South Africa.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF I have signed and sealed

these presents at Pretoría on

the twenty-secand day

of September One thousand Nine hundred and Fifty-one.

T7, Malan
